summary: Small stage decomp-matching PR for `src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c`: `grInishie1_801FBC4C` was reported as reaching 100% by introducing a local `HSD_JObj* jobj2` before calling `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP`, fixing register allocation.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; updated `grInishie1_801FBC4C` to store `gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2` in a local `HSD_JObj* jobj2` before `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: 'introducing a local `HSD_JObj* jobj2` to fix register allocation'

what_changed.change: Replaced direct macro argument `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2)` with a local assignment `HSD_JObj* jobj2 = gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2;` followed by `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(jobj2)`.
No semantic changes are evident in the diff after the macro call; the function still calls `Ground_801C4A08` on `hatena_gobj` and then clears `hatena_gobj` to `NULL`.

smart_moves.lesson: For a small matching delta, isolate the field load into a typed local before a macro call when register allocation is the stated blocker.
smart_moves.why_it_helped: The PR body says the local `HSD_JObj* jobj2` fixed register allocation, and the bot report shows `grInishie1_801FBC4C` moved from 93.85% to 100.00%.

smart_moves.lesson: Keep a focused PR limited to the single matching change needed for one function.
smart_moves.why_it_helped: The changed-file metadata shows one source file, one hunk, +2/-1 lines, and the bot report attributes one new match to `grInishie1_801FBC4C`.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a macro argument is a nested field access, introducing a typed local for that field can affect MWCC register allocation enough to close a match.
decomp_lessons.caveat: Evidence is one small PR; treat as a matching tactic to try, not a universal style rule.

naming_conventions.name: jobj2
naming_conventions.convention: The PR used a short local name matching the source field name `jobj2` for a `HSD_JObj*` temporary.

naming_conventions.name: gp
naming_conventions.convention: Existing local `Ground* gp = GET_GROUND(gobj);` remained unchanged; no new convention was introduced for ground pointers.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce a typed local from a nested struct/union field before passing it to a macro.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.verification_evidence: PR body claims 100%; decomp-dev bot report shows one new match for `main/melee/gr/grinishie1` item `grInishie1_801FBC4C`, before 93.85%, after 100.00%, +10 bytes.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 68.96% with 1 new match: `main/melee/gr/grinishie1` / `grInishie1_801FBC4C`, +10 bytes, 93.85% before, 100.00% after.
No loaded human review comments are present. The only human issue comment from the author says this was a small PR to test the waters and asks to be told if anything was missed.

follow_up_queries: DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP nested field local HSD_JObj register allocation

notes: The slice contains the PR body, changed-file metadata, full diff for the single hunk, and bot match report.
No review comments are loaded, so reviewer intent or human approval rationale cannot be inferred.
The raw PR JSON is truncated, but the needed identity, body, merge time, changed-file metadata, diff, and text corpus are available elsewhere in the loaded slice.
The PR evidence supports a local matching tactic, but it is only one function and should not be promoted as a broad rule without curator review.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed local before macro can fix register allocation
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2556, `grInishie1_801FBC4C` reached a bot-reported 100% match after changing `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2)` into a typed local `HSD_JObj* jobj2 = gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2; DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(jobj2);`. This is a narrow tactic to try when a nested field macro argument appears to affect register allocation.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting narrow local temporaries as a register-allocation matching tactic
source_update_candidates.text: A PR-local example shows that introducing a typed local for a nested field before a macro call can fix register allocation and close a match. This should remain evidence-gated and narrow, consistent with standards requiring verifier evidence for stack/register/local-lifetime tactics.
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standards discuss evidence for register/local tactics generally; this PR provides a concrete macro-argument local temporary example that may be useful as a source-owned example if the curator wants examples in standards.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP` calls should use locals; this PR only supports one instance in `grInishie1_801FBC4C`.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details; no loaded human review comments exist.
Do not promote broader claims about `hatena_gobj`, `Ground_801C4A08`, or Inishie stage behavior beyond the unchanged lines shown in the diff.
